# Stakeholder Register — <project name>

PMBOK Initiating / Planning artifact. Owned by `project-manager`.
Updates captured as new rows or `last_updated` bumps — do not delete
history, supersede.

## Identification

| ID | Name / role | Organization | Contact | Position on project |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S-1 | | | | |

## Assessment

| ID | Key requirements | Expectations | Influence | Phase of greatest interest | Engagement target |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| S-1 | | | high / med / low | initiating / planning / executing / closing | unaware / resistant / neutral / supportive / leading |

## Classification

| ID | Internal / external | Power | Interest |
|---|---|---|---|
| S-1 | | high / med / low | high / med / low |

## Engagement plan

For each stakeholder: communication cadence, channel, format, the
agent/teammate that owns the relationship (usually `tech-lead` for
customer, `project-manager` for internal coordination), and the
specific asks or commitments in play.

| ID | Cadence | Channel | Owner | Next ask / commitment |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S-1 | first-session-of-week / on-demand / milestone | email / chat / call | | |

Cadences are **session-anchored, run-once** — see `CLAUDE.md`
§ "Time-based cadences." "first-session-of-week" = the first
session opened on or after the project's calendar-week boundary
(the project picks which day starts the week — ISO 8601 Monday,
US Sunday, or whatever matches the customer's locale; record the
choice in `CUSTOMER_NOTES.md`). Missed weeks do not accumulate.
